text: Refactoring is the process of improving code structure without changing how it works. Its main goal is to make the code easier to read, understand, and maintain. For example, you might rename unclear variables or break a large function into smaller ones. The program’s behavior stays exactly the same. Over time, code can become messy and hard to manage if changes are made quickly. This creates something called 'technical debt', like borrowing time now but paying more later. Regular refactoring helps reduce this debt, keeping the code clean and saving time for future developers. It is an important habit for writing high-quality software.
